protein_name: a type 1 transmembrane protein that is a ligand for Notch receptors. Binds the extracellular domain of Notch receptor, inducing Notch signaling through the cleavage, hyperphosphorylation, and nuclear accumulation of the intracellular domain of Notch receptors (NICD). Notch signaling regulates cell lineage specification, cell patterning and morphogenesis through effects on differentiation and proliferation, and is required for embryonic development and maintenance of adult stem cells in many different tissues and immune system. Blocks the differentiation of progenitor cells into the B-cell lineage while promoting the emergence of a population of cells with the characteristics of a T-cell/NK-cell precursor. Plays a role during muscle and pancreatic cell development. Directly modulates cell adhesion and basal lamina formation in satellite cells through Notch signaling. Expressed in heart and pancreas, with lower expression in brain and muscle and almost no expression in placenta, lung, liver and kidney. 2 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
